expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Split N application; maintain P/K per soil test on strong loam.
expected_output.timing_advice: Plant when soil temperature stabilizes; favorable spring showers in forecast.
expected_output.reasoning: Good Iowa loam with solid nutrients and mild wet spring supports corn or rotation to soybean.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Conserve moisture with reduced tillage; fertigate lightly to match water.
expected_output.timing_advice: Irrigate at critical growth stages; avoid peak afternoon evaporation.
expected_output.reasoning: Dry hot forecast and low soil moisture require irrigation to protect yield on drought-tolerant crops.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Build with legume or small grain rotation before heavy corn again; lime if pH drifts high.
expected_output.timing_advice: Plan cover or rotation crop for next season; avoid continuous corn without amendment.
expected_output.reasoning: Soil test shows depleted macronutrients; rotating breaks pest cycles and restores fertility.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Use compost, approved organic amendments, and legume green manure only.
expected_output.timing_advice: Work fields when trafficable; manage drainage before heavy rains.
expected_output.reasoning: High organic matter organic field; nutrient strategy must stay within certified organic inputs.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Follow rice N schedule for flooded culture; verify zinc on heavy clay if needed.
expected_output.timing_advice: Flood and establish when temperatures support rice; warm humid window suits paddy prep.
expected_output.reasoning: Clay delta soil and irrigation suit flooded rice; weather is warm and wet for establishment.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Minimal N for wine quality; foliar or soil amendments only as petiole tests indicate.
expected_output.timing_advice: Deficit irrigate through veraison; avoid excess vigor on gravelly soils.
expected_output.reasoning: Perennial vineyard on coastal pattern: mild temps, low rain — manage water for canopy balance.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Topdress N in spring based on tiller count and yield goal; watch sulfur on silt.
expected_output.timing_advice: Monitor winter wheat for spring green-up; delay spring ops if soils too wet.
expected_output.reasoning: Winter wheat is in dormant/growth stages; timing centers on spring topdress and freeze protection.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Split nutrient on sand; integrate IPM scouting given regional pest history.
expected_output.timing_advice: Scout weekly during squaring/flowering; treat thresholds not calendars.
expected_output.reasoning: Warm humid summer increases pest risk; sandy acid soil needs careful fertility and monitoring.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Compost and OM-rich amendments for garden scale; avoid over-fertilizing small plots.
expected_output.timing_advice: Mulch and consistent water for tomatoes; stagger planting for short season.
expected_output.reasoning: Hobby plot with high OM loam; drip or hand watering may be needed without broad irrigation infrastructure.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Variable-rate N from grid samples; prioritize pivot zones on 1000+ acre operation.
expected_output.timing_advice: Coordinate planting windows across crews; use forecast for pre-plant herbicide timing.
expected_output.reasoning: Strong commercial loam with irrigation; large acreage needs precision ag and operational scheduling.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Match high rainfall with split applications and cover to reduce leaching.
expected_output.timing_advice: Year-round warmth suits tropical crops; plant after field prep for wet season drainage.
expected_output.reasoning: Tropical climate favors heat-loving crops like bananas; irrigation available for dry spells.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Build OM with cover crops; lime and band P/K; avoid broadcast loss on sand.
expected_output.timing_advice: Amend after harvest; plant cover to reduce erosion over winter.
expected_output.reasoning: Poor sandy soil benefits from organic matter and targeted amendment before next cash crop.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Rotate to soybean to reduce N need next cycle; adjust corn N if staying on corn.
expected_output.timing_advice: Transition plan: plant soybean this season or diversify to break pest cycle.
expected_output.reasoning: Transitioning from continuous corn benefits from rotation to soybean or alfalfa for soil and pest balance.

expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Plan spring soil test; no field application until soils thaw and drain.
expected_output.timing_advice: Do not plant corn in winter; schedule for late spring when soil temperature and frost risk allow.
expected_output.reasoning: Corn is not planted in Minnesota winter; wait for proper spring window despite planning interest.

risk_factors: Extreme heat and zero forecast rain
expected_output.fertilizer_recommendation: Defer planting until water strategy exists; avoid high-water crops without water rights.
expected_output.timing_advice: Do not establish water-intensive crops until irrigation or monsoon timing is secured.
expected_output.reasoning: Arid region forecast shows no rain; without irrigation, planting most row crops is high risk.
